skill.name: json-canvas
skill.description: Create and edit JSON Canvas files for Obsidian-compatible visual maps, flowcharts, boards, and connected note layouts.
skill.summary: Build valid .canvas diagrams with nodes, edges, groups, colors, and layout guidance.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are markdown examples, inline code formatting, hexadecimal IDs, and reference URLs in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, command execution instructions, credential handling, malicious network behavior, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: External Link and URL Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill describes JSON Canvas link nodes and references public specification pages. These URLs may cause generated canvases to include external links, but the skill does not fetch data or transmit information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced URLs are visible markdown/spec examples and JSON Canvas link-node fields. No network request code or hidden exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Local File Path References in Canvas Nodes
low_findings.description: The skill teaches file nodes that reference local vault paths and attachments. This can expose local path names inside generated canvas files, but it does not read files or access the filesystem by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path fields are part of JSON Canvas examples and are not used by executable code. The residual risk is disclosure through user-requested output.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Ruby Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis treated markdown inline code and fenced JSON examples as Ruby backtick execution. SKILL.md is documentation and contains no Ruby code, shell commands, or executable helper files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens appear in markdown prose, tables, and JSON code fences. There is no interpreter context where these backticks could execute.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Hex IDs Misread as Weak Cryptography
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged many 16-character example identifiers and the ID generation section as weak cryptographic usage. The skill only documents canvas object IDs and does not implement cryptography.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged strings are sample node and edge IDs in JSON examples. No hashing, encryption, or authentication code is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: C2 Keywords in Benign Diagram Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis reported C2 keyword blockers in example canvas content and identifiers. Review found no command-and-control instructions, persistence logic, remote execution, or suspicious endpoint behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is JSON Canvas sample data for research and flowchart diagrams. The file contains no operational malware behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need valid canvas files that work in Obsidian and related tools. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build structured .canvas maps with nodes, edges, groups, and layout rules.
actual_capabilities: Creates valid JSON Canvas structures with nodes and edges.
Supports text, file, link, and group node types.
Defines edge labels, sides, arrows, colors, and endpoints.
Provides layout guidance for spacing, sizing, and positioning.
Includes examples for mind maps, project boards, research maps, and flowcharts.
limitations: Does not render or preview canvas files visually.
Does not validate files with an external JSON Canvas parser.
Does not read local attachments referenced by file nodes.
Requires the user to provide content and desired layout intent.

use_cases.title: Plan an Obsidian Knowledge Map
use_cases.description: Create a connected canvas that organizes notes, questions, resources, and next steps.

use_cases.title: Draft a Product Workflow
use_cases.description: Turn a process description into a flowchart with clear steps, decisions, and labeled paths.

use_cases.title: Build a Visual Project Board
use_cases.description: Generate grouped canvas sections for tasks, priorities, status, and supporting files.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Canvas
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple visual map.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a JSON Canvas file for this topic: [topic]. Include one central idea, four supporting ideas, and labeled connections.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Notes to Canvas
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these notes into a .canvas structure. Use text nodes for ideas, file nodes for referenced notes, and edges for relationships: [notes].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Process Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced layout request for a workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a JSON Canvas flowchart for this process. Include decision points, arrows, labels, colors, and readable spacing: [process].

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Canvas
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced editing request for an existing canvas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this canvas structure and improve the layout, grouping, edge labels, and validation issues without changing the core content: [canvas description].

output_examples.input: A user asks for a research map about renewable energy storage.
output_examples.output: A canvas plan with a central research topic, linked source nodes, question nodes, diagram placeholders, and labeled evidence connections.

output_examples.input: A user asks to turn a launch checklist into a visual workflow.
output_examples.output: A flowchart-style canvas with start and end nodes, review steps, decision branches, and color-coded status groups.

output_examples.input: A user asks for an Obsidian project board.
output_examples.output: A grouped board layout with planning, active, blocked, and completed sections plus task cards and supporting file references.

best_practices: Provide the topic, desired canvas style, and important relationships before generation.
Ask for validation of node IDs, required fields, and edge references before using the file.
Use meaningful labels and consistent spacing so the canvas remains readable.
anti_patterns: Do not request a large canvas without grouping or layout constraints.
Do not include private local paths unless they are safe to store in the canvas file.
Do not treat generated link nodes as verified or trusted sources.

faq.question: What is JSON Canvas?
faq.answer: JSON Canvas is an open format for storing infinite canvas diagrams as JSON files.

faq.question: Can this skill create Obsidian canvas files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create .canvas structures that follow the JSON Canvas format used by Obsidian.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing canvas?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the current structure or a clear description of the changes you need.

faq.question: Does it render the canvas visually?
faq.answer: No. It creates the underlying canvas structure for another application to display.

faq.question: Can it include files and links?
faq.answer: Yes. It can define file nodes and link nodes, but it does not verify that targets exist.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the topic, node content, relationships, preferred layout, and any required file or link references.
